title: Level 642

{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 642: “Will It Ever Stop?”}} {{CSS|content=.backgroundplate { filter: blur(7px); }}} {{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(84, 69, 28, 0); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgb(194, 189, 163); --theme-link-color: #918751; --theme-community-header-color:#654321; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: rgb(56, 51, 36); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: rgb(181, 168, 130); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: rgb(181, 168, 130); } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgb(20, 20, 20); --theme-link-color: #ccb36e; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: rgb(56, 51, 36); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: rgb(181, 168, 130); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: rgb(181, 168, 130); } } }}{{Background|file=Level 642.jpg}}

'''Description'''
{{Blurquote |image=Level 642.jpg |quote-text=
1 foot. 2 feet. 3 feet. When does it stop?
}}
Level 642 is the 643th level within the Backrooms.
[[File:Level 642.jpg|link=|355px|The Start..?]]
A picture of '''Level 642''', showing a basement.
'''Level 642, also known as "Will It Ever Stop?" or "Contaminated Basements", is described as a large complex of interconnected basements and underground garages. These areas typically resemble ordinary house basements with seemingly no end. Despite the vast size of the complex, no staircases or ladders have been observed. The basements usually remain partially filled with water at the beginning of exploration. Despite this, there are no visible signs of interior or exterior water damage.''' '''The level itself does not appear to be actively affected by the water, though signs of material decay have been observed. This deterioration primarily affects old oak wood used for structural support, including both vertical and horizontal pillars. The majority of the level is constructed of stone, reinforced concrete, and standard concrete, with additional wooden beams integrated into the structure.''' '''The lighting system of Level 642 is poor, typically flickering and occasionally shutting off entirely. The lights are generally integrated into the wooden ceiling. They are encased in unbreakable glass, with no known method of damaging or breaking them. The lights emit approximately 1200 lumens, producing a bright white glow that is visible from other rooms.''' '''The basements are also filled with a wide variety of items and furnitures. These things are usually found in basement, old moving boxes, closets, sofas, etc. The wanderer may also encounter gas cans. These gas cans are filled to the brim with highly flammeable gasoline or other flammable liquids. You should not try to ignite the liquids as it's impossible to put out. Splashing water onto the fire will not help and would actually intensity the flame. This liquid may be useful in other levels; however, it remains highly lethal and must be handled with extreme caution.''' '''Wanderers may encounter small closet-like rooms scattered throughout the level. These rooms are typically empty or contain very few objects. They measure approximately 2 × 4 × 7 feet and are primarily constructed of wood and concrete, unlike the level’s general structural layout. Notably, these rooms contain no water whatsoever. Water is unable to enter them under any observed circumstances.''' '''Despite their dry conditions, wanderers are strongly advised against resting inside these rooms. As the surrounding water level continues to rise, exiting may become impossible, potentially resulting in entrapment.''' '''The general temperature of the level remains at approximately 55°F (12.7°C), though minor variations may occur between rooms. The overall water level also appears to influence ambient temperature. Closet-like rooms tend to remain slightly warmer, averaging around 62°F (17°C). In contrast, the water itself maintains a significantly colder temperature of approximately 44°F (7°C).''' '''This level is also subject to [[Phenomenon 15]], which further intensifies the overall sense of anxiety experienced by wanderers.'''
'''Environment & Properties'''
'''Level 642’s environment is extremely lethal. The level is constantly covered in water, which serves as its primary hazard. This water is inhabited by brain-eating parasites. Additionally, the water level rises at a rate of 5 cm per hour, making prolonged exposure exceptionally dangerous.''' === 30-40 Minutes === '''After approximately 30 minutes within the level, wanderers will begin to notice the increasing water level. At this stage, it does not pose an immediate threat. The parasites remain non-aggressive and will not actively attack. Small debris may begin to float, and some doors may become difficult to open due to water pressure.''' '''At this stage, the water level measures approximately 7 cm in depth.''' === 1-2 Hour(s) === '''After approximately 1–2 hours within the level, wanderers will begin to notice medium-sized furniture starting to float, and doors will become nearly impossible to open. The parasites will gradually become more aggressive and may begin attacking wanderers. The walls may also begin to crack due to debris repeatedly striking them.''' '''At this stage, the water level typically reaches approximately 10–14 cm in depth.''' === 2-4 Hours === '''After approximately 2–4 hours within the level, wanderers will observe that the water has risen to roughly knee height. Clothing may become dirty or heavily soiled due to prolonged exposure to the contaminated water.''' '''At this stage, furniture weighing more than 200 pounds (90.7 kg) may begin to float slightly or become partially submerged. Doors will be nearly impossible to open without assistance; the use of a crowbar or other metal tools is strongly recommended.''' '''Wanderers are advised not to remain submerged in the water for more than five minutes at a time, as the parasites will begin attacking any exposed organic material.''' '''At this stage, the water level measures approximately 20–23 cm in depth.''' === 4-6 Hours === '''After approximately 4–6 hours within the level, the environment becomes significantly more lethal. Large furniture will begin to float freely, and movement will become increasingly difficult for wanderers.''' '''Some walls may become partially destroyed or show large structural cracks caused by repeated debris impacts. The parasites begin to aggregate into unusual orange clusters visible in the water. Wanderers are strongly advised to avoid contact with these formations.''' '''By this stage, the ambient temperature drops to approximately 39°F (4°C), heavily influenced by the increasing volume of cold water.''' '''At this stage, the water level has risen to approximately 28–32 cm.''' === 7 Hours+ === '''If a wanderer remains within the level beyond the 7-hour mark, the situation becomes critical. By this stage, very large furniture such as sofas, wardrobes, smokers, workbenches, or any object weighing more than 400 pounds (181.4 kg) will begin floating freely. These objects may collide with walls and doors, causing severe structural damage and, in some cases, complete collapse.''' '''The orange parasite clusters will continue spreading, forming along the remaining walls and across sections of the ceiling. Horizontal support beams may begin to fail, with many eventually breaking under sustained impact and water pressure.''' '''At this point, the water level will have risen to approximately 40 cm or higher.'''
'''Entities & Dangers'''
'''Although Level 642 is largely devoid of entities, it remains inhabited by [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], both male and female. They are most commonly observed in larger, open basement sections or in partially flooded areas.''' '''The parasite present within the water appears to be a hybridized strain resembling the [[Hydrolitis Plague]] combined with an unidentified rust-like mutation. Noticeable spread typically begins between the 4–6 hour mark, at which point the organism aggregates into large orange clusters. These growths adhere to walls and ceilings, particularly in areas where structural decay is already present.'''
'''Colonies & Outposts'''
'''Due to the continuously rising water and the level’s exposure to [[Phenomenon 15]], no permanent colonies or outposts have been successfully established.'''
'''Entrances and Exits'''
===Entrances=== *'''Noclipping through a metal door on [[Level 19]] may lead here. *'''Finding a basement within a house on [[Level 69]] has a low chance of leading here. *'''Wandering far enough within [[Level 477]] may result in arrival at this level. *'''Touching an orange box within [[Level 20]] will lead here. ===Exits=== *'''Finding a doll inside a closet may send one to [[Level 249]]. *'''Opening a manhole cover and entering it may lead to [[Level 34]]. *'''A trapdoor will lead to [[Level 297]].
